name: Aiea Heights
desc: Hillside above Aiea — ridge trail access, Pearl Harbor views, well-maintained older homes, local feel.
insider: The Aiea Loop Trail trailhead at the top of Aiea Heights Drive is a hidden gem — arrive before 8am on weekends to snag a parking spot
Views of Pearl Harbor and the Waianae Mountains from the ridge are breathtaking, especially at sunrise when the light hits the harbor
Keaiwa Heiau State Park at the top has tent camping, picnic pavilions, and a Hawaiian medicinal garden showcasing native plants used for traditional healing
The neighborhood is very dog-friendly — you'll see people walking at all hours on the quiet residential streets
Wild pigs occasionally wander onto upper streets from the forest — don't leave pet food outside overnight
The elevation keeps temperatures a few degrees cooler than sea-level Aiea, and the trade winds are noticeably stronger up here
Most homes were built in the 1960s-70s and are solidly constructed — but check for termite history before buying
The Aiea Loop Trail connects to the ridgeline above and on clear days you can see from Barbers Point to Diamond Head
Street parking is limited near the trailhead — if the lot is full, don't block driveways or residents will call HPD
This was once part of Aiea Sugar Plantation land — the park's eucalyptus and Norfolk pine trees were planted by plantation workers in the 1920s
